other_observers: Jeff Dunning and Andy Boyce; I'm the original finder of the bird.
specific_location: Last Chance Rest Area Mucky Puddle
description: Structurally a warbler.   Very slightly larger than the several nearby Common Yellowthroats (COYE)  Olive-drab back color same as COYEs; extends up on to neck and the back of the head  Bright yellow throat color same as COYEs; however the yellow in the throat extended all the way down over the belly and through the vent area  Yellow eyebrow extending from behind the eye to the bill  Black on top of head which wraps around the yellow eyebrow and over the dark eye and also drops down towards the neck  Heavier bill than the COYEsBehaviors: Skulking  I stood very quietly on the north side of that nasty Last Chance puddle hoping something good might come in for a drink. There were at least six COYE present in the area. I almost dismissed the KEWA as another COYE as it failed to stand out initially.  It stayed very low to the ground and skirted the south edges of the puddle disappearing into the undergrowth several times.Call: N/APlumage: Adult
